front: Ruler: Communist dictator of Romania executed by firing squad alongside his wife on Christmas Day 1989
Ruler: longtime Communist dictator/President of Romania, ruling from 1967 until his overthrow in 1989
Ruler: became General Secretary of Romania's Communist Party after the death of his mentor Gheorghe Gheorghiu-Dej
Ruler: rose to power aided by the purge of Ana Pauker and the "Muscovite" faction within Romania's Communist Party
Ruler: met his predecessor Gheorghe Gheorghiu-Dej while both were imprisoned at Targu Jiu during World War II
Law: banned abortion and contraception in Romania (1966), declaring "the fetus is the property of the entire society"
Law: declared women who bore ten or more children "heroine mothers" (with an "Order of Maternal Glory" for seven or more)
Law: threatened doctors with pay cuts if birth rates fell in their district
Law: led to unwanted children ("decretei") being crammed into brutal state orphanages such as Cighid and Siret
Law: led orphanage staff to give malnourished children blood transfusions, seeding an AIDS epidemic
Figure: pushed the "heroine mothers" campaign through her National Women's Council
Ruler: Freakonomics authors Levitt and Dubner argued Romania's abandoned "decretei" generation grew up to overthrow this dictator in 1989
Document: 1971 speech, influenced by Kim Il Sung and Mao Zedong, urging an intensification of socialist ideology and education of the masses
Ruler: adopted self-given titles like "Genius of the Carpathians" as part of his personality cult
Ruler: Salvador Dali sent him a sarcastic congratulatory telegram for "introducing the presidential sceptre," which his own newspaper printed
Concept: pseudo-historical theory claiming the Tartaria Tables depicted the first alphabet, promoted by intellectuals under this leader
Institution: secret police founded in 1948 by Gheorghe Pintilie, employed by this dictator, and abolished shortly after his 1989 overthrow
Ruler: intelligence officer Ion Pacepa defected from the Securitate during his rule
Ruler: his regime suppressed the independent trade union SLOMR, backed by dissident Paul Goma
Event: 1977 strike by workers demanding this Romanian dictator's deposition
Event: document in which the opposition denounced this Romanian dictator
Ruler: a group working for him allegedly used a device called the "radu," meant to induce cancer in targets
Ruler: refused to join the 1968 Warsaw Pact invasion of Czechoslovakia during the Prague Spring, publicly denouncing it
Ruler: Richard Nixon's 1969 visit to him was the first by a U.S. president to a Communist country since the start of the Cold War
Ruler: secured a contract to build Rolls-Royce Spey engines for the BAC One-Eleven, then was forced to repay the collapsed deal's debt in strawberries
Event: urban-renewal policy that razed much of historic Bucharest, inspiring a neologism blending this dictator's name with Hiroshima
Event: redesigned Bucharest's Civic Center district, including nearly a mile of fountains along what is now Union Boulevard
Event: had historic churches such as the Antim monastery relocated on giant rails to clear space for construction
Building: construction materials from across Romania were used almost exclusively to build this huge, mostly unoccupied administrative structure, inspired by a visit to Pyongyang
Building: also called the People's House, reputedly the world's heaviest building
Ruler: Dacia cars were first manufactured under his rule
Ruler: an earlier coup against him failed after the army unit tasked with attacking him was instead sent to harvest maize
Ruler: protesters reportedly signaled his impending fall by hanging pears on apple trees
Ruler: the revolution against him began after an attempted eviction of pastor Laszlo Tokes from his church in Timisoara sparked protests
Ruler: his crackdown on protesters in Timisoara, met with water cannons and tear gas, led to genocide charges against him
Ruler: his final speech was overshadowed by crowds chanting "Timisoara!"
Ruler: fled Bucharest by helicopter with his wife before being captured during the 1989 revolution
Ruler: was given a hasty trial (a drumhead court-martial resembling a kangaroo court) on charges including genocide before his execution
Ruler: Ion Iliescu took power in Romania after his execution
Ruler: was exhumed in 2010 to perform DNA tests, but was actually identified by the black coat he was wearing at death
